untranslatable_reason: Signal requires option-specific parameters (ATM strike identification, premium calculation, expected move at expiration) that cannot be expressed with equity price/technical indicators alone. Straddle pricing logic, breakeven thresholds based on strike ± premium, and implied volatility comparisons to realized volatility are not available in the allowed indicator set. This is an options strategy that requires derivatives pricing framework, not equity bar data.
translation_notes: NATENBERG_DRY_9_0 is a volatility-based options straddle strategy. While HIGH_VOL regime can be approximated using hvol_20, the core logic—comparing expected move (implied vol derived) to premium paid (option Greeks), identifying ATM strikes, and calculating strike-relative breakevens—requires an options-specific backtesting engine with access to implied volatility surfaces, option pricing models, and Greeks. The fundamental rule structure cannot be translated to equity price action indicators.
